Risk Title: Pre-Development Delays Cascading to UAT
Risk Description: Because of consistent delays in Pre-Development deliverables (User Story/UX finalization, Functional Requirements, HLD approvals) across modules 0.2-0.8 as documented in QBR and MBR, there is a risk that development start dates will continue to slip and rework will be required, which could lead to cascading delays in UAT timelines, missed Release 1 go-live dates, and potential contractual penalties.
Tech Stack: .NET 8, Angular 18, SQL Server 2019, Azure, AWS
Potential Impact: Delayed UAT and go-live dates for Release 1, potential contractual violations, increased project costs due to extended timelines, client dissatisfaction

Financial Metric: AWS Bedrock Claude Sonnet 4.5 costs approved
Risk Title: Infrastructure Dependency Blocking AI Development
Risk Description: Because of critical infrastructure dependencies requiring AWS setup completion, FMS configuration, and entire pipeline/ADO to AWS integration as documented in QBR Issue log and MBR dependencies, there is a risk that Billie Part 2 development and UAT will be delayed by at least 4 weeks beyond infrastructure readiness, which could lead to Release 1 scope reduction, delayed invoice processing automation, and inability to meet Phase 2 contractual milestones.
Tech Stack: AWS Bedrock, Claude Sonnet 4.5, LLM, OCR, Python, C#, Azure DevOps
Potential Impact: Delayed AI-powered invoice processing, Release 1 milestone miss, potential scope reduction, contractual penalties, delayed ROI from automation

Task/Item: Reporting Center and Power BI Integration
Risk Title: Reporting Infrastructure and Requirements Blocking Delivery
Risk Description: Because of dual blockers including incomplete infrastructure setup (Data gateway to Power BI Service, Power BI Workspace configuration) and pending requirements for standard reports and reporting center as documented in QBR Critical Issues, there is a risk that the Reporting module development timeline cannot be determined and UAT will be significantly delayed, which could lead to inability to deliver financial statements, compliance reporting gaps, and delayed business intelligence capabilities for Phase 2 go-live.
Tech Stack: Power BI, Azure Data Gateway, Angular, .NET APIs, SQL Server
Potential Impact: Missing critical reporting capabilities at go-live, compliance reporting gaps, delayed business intelligence, inability to generate financial statements, potential audit issues

Risk Title: Defect Leakage Risk from Retesting Cycles
Risk Description: Because of recurring defect reopenings documented in MBR (44 total bugs as of Feb 24, with reopen trends showing 1-5 defects per sprint) and historical defect leakage of ~3-4% mentioned in context, there is a risk that defect leakage could exceed the 5% threshold during intensive UAT phases, which could lead to delayed releases, increased retesting costs, and client-reported production defects impacting system stability.
Tech Stack: JIRA, QA Automation Tools, Selenium, Postman
Potential Impact: Increased retesting effort and costs, potential UAT delays, risk of production defects, client dissatisfaction, extended stabilization period

SLA Metric: Development started with partial HLD approval
Risk Title: Development Rework Risk from Incomplete Approvals
Risk Description: Because development started on July 17, 2025 with only HLD 7.2 approved while Approval Engine HLD (Kannan, ETA 8/1) and Approval UI HLD (Keith, ETA 7/30) are still pending approval as documented in QBR Critical Issues, there is a risk that significant rework will be required when final HLDs are approved, which could lead to wasted development effort, timeline slippage, increased costs, and potential architectural inconsistencies.
Tech Stack: .NET 8, Angular 18, Azure, SQL Server, Microservices
Potential Impact: Wasted development effort requiring rework, timeline delays, increased project costs, potential architectural inconsistencies, team morale impact

SLA Metric: Development delayed - revised start date TBD
Risk Title: Payment Module Delivery Risk from Pre-Development Delays
Risk Description: Because of incomplete Pre-Development deliverables requiring Functional Requirements approval (7/31), Design approval (8/8), Data Model approval (8/1), and HLD approval (TBD) as documented in QBR Critical Issues with original development start date of July 15, 2025 already missed, there is a risk that the Payments/Disbursements module will face extended delays and compressed development/testing cycles, which could lead to rushed implementation, quality issues, and potential payment processing errors in production.
Tech Stack: .NET 8, Payment APIs, Banking Integration, Azure
Potential Impact: Critical payment processing delays, compressed testing cycles increasing defect risk, potential payment errors in production, business operation disruption

Risk Title: Vendor Module Delay from User Research Dependencies
Risk Description: Because of incomplete user research requiring updates to deliverables with Functional Requirements, Design, Data Model, and HLD approvals all pending (target 8/1-8/8) as documented in QBR Critical Issues, and original development start date of July 21 already passed with revised start date of August 4, there is a risk that the Vendor module will face compressed timelines and inadequate user validation, which could lead to poor user experience, vendor management inefficiencies, and potential rework after UAT feedback.

Potential Impact: Compressed development timeline, inadequate user validation, potential poor UX requiring rework, vendor management inefficiencies, UAT delays

Risk Title: Environment Downtime Impacting Development Productivity
Risk Description: Because of recurring application downtime issues resulting in 181 hours of team productivity loss since May 14, 2025 as documented in QBR Critical Risk with environment issues tracked in Confluence, there is a risk that continued infrastructure instability will cause further productivity losses and development delays, which could lead to missed sprint commitments, extended project timelines, increased costs, and team frustration.
Tech Stack: Azure, AWS, Enterprise Services, Network Infrastructure
Potential Impact: Continued productivity losses, missed sprint commitments, extended project timelines, increased costs, team morale issues, potential data loss

Risk Title: UI Revamp Schedule Risk Impacting User Experience
Risk Description: Because of the comprehensive UI revamp using Play+ framework requiring two implementation phases (Phase 1: 7/23-8/7 for installation and testing, Phase 2: 8/11-9/8 for updating existing screens) as documented in QBR Risks, there is a risk that UI changes will introduce regression issues or user experience problems across all modules, which could lead to UAT delays, user adoption challenges, and potential need to rollback or rework UI components.
Tech Stack: Angular 18, Play+ Framework, .NET 8, UI/UX Tools
Potential Impact: Potential UI regression issues, user experience problems, UAT delays, user adoption challenges, possible rollback or rework of UI components

Risk Title: Performance Testing Gaps Threatening SLA Compliance
Risk Description: Because of pending performance testing discussions scheduled for 3/3 with test plan development still in progress, and stringent performance requirements (UI page rendering <=10 seconds, API response <=3 seconds) documented in MBR Project Scorecard and Risks, there is a risk that performance testing will be inadequate or delayed, which could lead to inability to meet performance SLAs, production performance issues, user dissatisfaction, and potential need for infrastructure upgrades or code optimization.

Potential Impact: Inability to meet performance SLAs, production performance degradation, user dissatisfaction, potential infrastructure upgrade costs, code optimization rework

Risk Title: Data Migration Strategy Uncertainty Threatening Go-Live
Risk Description: Because of unknown data migration strategy and estimates with uncertainty around bringing 5 beta customers ready at go-live as documented in MBR Risks (Low probability, High severity), there is a risk that data migration planning and execution will be inadequate, which could lead to delayed customer onboarding, data integrity issues, inability to migrate legacy Zenith data to NexLedgr, and potential need for manual data entry as last resort.
Tech Stack: Python, C#, SQL Server, Data Migration Tools, ETL
Potential Impact: Delayed customer onboarding, data integrity issues, inability to migrate legacy data, potential manual data entry effort, go-live delays

Risk Title: Security Vulnerabilities from Incomplete Security Testing
Risk Description: Because of pending action on BurpSuite security scan results documented in MBR Open Items (opened 12/18, TBD close date) and internal security review in progress per Project Scorecard, there is a risk that security vulnerabilities will not be addressed before production deployment, which could lead to security breaches, data exposure, compliance violations, and potential financial and reputational damage.
Tech Stack: BurpSuite, .NET 8, Azure Security, SQL Server, RBAC
Potential Impact: Security breaches, data exposure, compliance violations, financial penalties, reputational damage, potential production deployment blocks

Risk Title: API Regression Coverage Gap Increasing Defect Risk
Risk Description: Because of low API regression automation coverage at 22% overall with 300+ APIs requiring automation as documented in MBR Open Items (target 3/16 for agent-based automation POC), there is a risk that API changes will introduce undetected regressions and integration issues, which could lead to production defects, system integration failures, and increased manual testing effort and costs.

Potential Impact: Undetected API regressions, integration failures in production, increased manual testing effort and costs, potential system outages

Risk Title: API Security Architecture Risk from Incomplete Categorization
Risk Description: Because of incomplete public vs private API categorization requiring follow-up sessions with Keith and pending comprehensive list of background processes with API endpoints (ETA 3/9) as documented in MBR Dependencies, there is a risk that API security boundaries will be improperly implemented, which could lead to unauthorized API access, security vulnerabilities, compliance issues, and potential data breaches.

Potential Impact: Unauthorized API access, security vulnerabilities, compliance violations, potential data breaches, architectural rework required

Risk Title: AAVA AI Tool Availability Risk Impacting Productivity
Risk Description: Because of pending AAVA 2.0 infrastructure issues including DNS changes causing UI problems and AKS cluster upgrade to 1.33 required by 3/31 as documented in MBR AAVA 2.0 Progress section, there is a risk that the AI development tool will have limited availability or functionality, which could lead to reduced development productivity (40% productivity gain at risk per QBR), increased manual coding effort, and slower delivery velocity.

SLA Metric: 19 UI screens, 59 APIs for Rollfi integration
Risk Title: Payroll Integration Dependency Risk
Risk Description: Because of dependency on third-party provider Rollfi for payroll functionality requiring integration of 19 UI/UX screens and 59 APIs as documented in SOW Phase 2 Amendment Schedule A, and potential for Zenith to change providers requiring change management process, there is a risk that payroll integration will face delays or require rework if Rollfi cooperation is inadequate or provider changes occur, which could lead to incomplete payroll functionality at go-live, manual payroll processing requirements, and delayed employee payment capabilities.
Tech Stack: Rollfi APIs, .NET 8, Angular 18, Integration APIs, JSON
Potential Impact: Incomplete payroll functionality at go-live, manual payroll processing requirements, delayed employee payment capabilities, potential provider change rework

Owner: Axos Bank Team, Ascendion Integration Team
Risk Title: Banking Integration Functional Parity Risk
Risk Description: Because Axos Bank is primarily handling integration of banking products and services with Ascendion providing assistance to verify functional parity with Current Zenith System as documented in SOW Phase 2 Amendment, there is a risk that banking integration gaps will not be identified until late in development or during UAT, which could lead to incomplete banking functionality, gaps requiring fixes through change management process, and delayed core banking operations for customers.

Potential Impact: Incomplete banking functionality, functional parity gaps, late-stage fixes requiring change management, delayed core banking operations

Financial Metric: Fixed price for Releases 1-3, Optional Release 4
SLA Metric: Change management via fixed price, hourly rate, or 300 Accrued Hours
Risk Title: Scope Creep Risk Under Fixed Price Model
Risk Description: Because Phase 2 operates under fixed price contract for Releases 1-3 with change management options (fixed price quote, $60-120/hour, or 300 Accrued Hours with 25 hours/month limit) as documented in SOW Phase 2 Amendment Schedule B, there is a risk that scope changes will exceed Accrued Hours allocation or require additional budget approvals, which could lead to project budget overruns, delayed implementation of critical changes, contractual disputes, and reduced project flexibility.
Tech Stack: Contract Management, Change Control Process
Potential Impact: Project budget overruns, delayed critical changes, contractual disputes, reduced flexibility, potential scope reduction to stay within budget

Risk Title: Extended Timeline Increasing Cost and Resource Risk
Risk Description: Because overall project timeline has been extended to mid-July/August 2026 timeframe per Decision D-002 approved by Marc as documented in MBR Key Decisions, and multiple modules face ongoing delays, there is a risk that further timeline extensions will be required, which could lead to increased project costs, resource burnout, opportunity costs from delayed ROI, competitive disadvantage, and potential contract renegotiation requirements.
Tech Stack: Program Management, Resource Management
Potential Impact: Increased project costs, resource burnout and attrition, delayed ROI, competitive disadvantage, potential contract renegotiation, stakeholder confidence erosion

Risk Title: UAT Quality Risk from Incomplete Readiness Assessment
Risk Description: Because UAT for releases 0.4+0.8 was paused per Decision D-005 requiring complete UAT readiness assessment from 0.1 through 0.4 before re-release to Axos as documented in MBR Key Decisions, there is a risk that modules will be released to UAT without adequate quality validation, which could lead to high defect rates in UAT, client dissatisfaction, repeated UAT cycles, and delayed production deployment.
Tech Stack: QA Tools, JIRA, Test Management, Azure DevOps
Potential Impact: High UAT defect rates, client dissatisfaction, repeated UAT cycles, delayed production deployment, erosion of client confidence

SLA Metric: Multiple features deferred from R1 to R2

Potential Impact: User dissatisfaction with limited R1 functionality, manual workarounds required, delayed business value, potential adoption resistance, competitive disadvantage

SLA Metric: Velocity variations across teams (Alpha: 426 SP, Cypher+Nexus: 684 SP, Apex: 125 SP)

Risk Description: Because of rapid development pace with defect density at 22.19 bugs per KLOC (significantly above expected 2 bugs per KLOC) as documented in MBR Project Scorecard, despite good code coverage (UI 85%, API 80%), there is a risk that technical debt is accumulating faster than it can be addressed, which could lead to increased maintenance costs, system instability, difficulty implementing future enhancements, and potential need for refactoring efforts post-deployment.
